response.description_long:
Relaymark automates the lifecycle messaging that e-commerce brands otherwise assemble by hand: welcome series, abandoned-cart recovery, win-back campaigns, and post-purchase flows across email and SMS. It connects to Shopify and BigCommerce, builds a profile of every shopper from orders and browsing events, and runs journeys from a visual builder where flows branch on profile data and live behavior — opened but did not click, clicked but did not buy — with exit rules that end a journey the moment its goal is met.

Measurement is the product's sharpest edge. Every flow runs against a holdout, so reported revenue is true incremental revenue rather than last-click attribution — the site calls attribution theater the industry disease and publishes aggregate incremental results per flow type quarterly. Send-time optimization runs on the same discipline, picking each shopper's likely engagement hour with lift measured against a control rather than asserted.

Traction is substantial: more than 9,000 brands run on Relaymark, including Lumeria, and the template library encodes what works — a 4-message abandoned-cart series is cited as the most-copied template — with every flow able to A/B test subject lines, timing, and offers. Templates lean into the verticals where customers concentrate: browse-recovery keyed to size for fashion, replenishment timed to consumption windows for beauty, reorder reminders for food and beverage.

Deliverability is treated as product surface rather than support ticket: dedicated sending infrastructure, warm-up schedules for new domains, list hygiene tooling, and automatic suppression of unengaged addresses before they hurt the sender score, with a deliverability specialist reviewing every account's first month of sends. SMS is handled with the care the channel demands — consent capture, quiet hours by recipient timezone, frequency caps, and coordination inside one journey so the cart reminder texts only if the email went unopened.

The edges round out the pitch: audience sync to Meta and Google ad platforms for suppression and lookalikes, webhooks streaming every message event to the warehouse, and review-request flows through Okendo and Judge.me. Migration is included on every plan — the team rebuilds existing flows during onboarding, typically inside two weeks, which matters in a category where switching costs are mostly accumulated flows.

Pricing scales with contactable profiles, email and SMS metered separately, with a free tier under 500 profiles. Relaymark is a 30-person company founded in 2020, fully remote, with customers concentrated in fashion, beauty, and food & beverage — a team drawn from retention managers and deliverability engineers who ran these programs by hand.
